View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Split a graph’s nodes into two equally-sized groups so that as few edges as possible cross between the groups. Think of dividing a school into two teams where friends want to stay together — minimise the number of friendships that get split.

The test uses planted graphs that can be perfectly bisected with zero crossing edges, so grading is strict pass/fail. Subpasses increase the graph size.

 1.0000
[tiny: 40 vertices] PASS: minimum cut 0 in 0.11s

Graph Bisection - tiny: 40 vertices

Vertices: 40 | Edges: ~108 | Target partition size: 20 | Known optimum cut: 0

View Rust Code (4771 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read};

// Compute the cut size for a given fixed assignment of vertices to A(0) / B(1)
fn compute_cut_for_assignment(assign: &[i8], edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> usize {
    edges
        .iter()
        .filter(|&&(u, v)| assign[u] != assign[v])
        .count()
}

struct Solver {
    n: usize,
    adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>,
    edges: Vec<(usize, usize)>,
    order: Vec<usize>,
    assign: Vec<i8>,       // current partial assignment during DFS (-1 unassigned, 0 -> A, 1 -> B)
    best_cut: usize,
    best_assign: Vec<i8>,    // best full assignment found (0/1 per vertex)
}

impl Solver {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, edges: Vec<(usize, usize)>, order: Vec<usize>, m: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            n,
            adj,
            edges,
            order,
            assign: vec![-1; n],
            best_cut: m,                 // an upper bound; will be lowered by an initial heuristic
            best_assign: vec![-1; n],
        }
    }

    // Depth-first search with pruning
    fn dfs(&mut self, idx: usize, a_count: usize, current_cut: usize) {
        // Prune if already worse than best known
        if current_cut >= self.best_cut {
            return;
        }
        if idx == self.n {
            // All vertices assigned; ensure exactly n/2 in A
            if a_count == self.n / 2 {
                self.best_cut = current_cut;
                self.best_assign = self.assign.clone();
            }
            return;
        }

        let v = self.order[idx];

        // Try assigning v to A (0)
        if a_count < self.n / 2 {
            // Count how many edges to already assigned B-nodes (these will cross)
            let mut delta = 0usize;
            for &u in &self.adj[v] {
                if self.assign[u] == 1 {
                    delta += 1;
                }
            }
            self.assign[v] = 0;
            self.dfs(idx + 1, a_count + 1, current_cut + delta);
            self.assign[v] = -1;
        }

        // Try assigning v to B (1)
        let b_count = idx - a_count;
        if b_count < self.n / 2 {
            // Count crossing edges to already assigned A-nodes
            let mut delta = 0usize;
            for &u in &self.adj[v] {
                if self.assign[u] == 0 {
                    delta += 1;
                }
            }
            self.assign[v] = 1;
            self.dfs(idx + 1, a_count, current_cut + delta);
            self.assign[v] = -1;
        }
    }

    fn solve(&mut self) {
        // Create a reasonable initial best solution: greedy split by vertex index
        // A gets first n/2 vertices; B gets the rest
        let mut init_assign = vec![-1i8; self.n];
        for i in 0..self.n {
            init_assign[i] = if i < self.n / 2 { 0 } else { 1 };
        }
        let init_cut = compute_cut_for_assignment(&init_assign, &self.edges);

        // Seed the search with the greedy solution
        self.best_cut = init_cut;
        self.best_assign = init_assign.clone();

        // Prepare for DFS: start with all unassigned
        for i in 0..self.n {
            self.assign[i] = -1;
        }

        self.dfs(0, 0, 0);
    }
}

fn main() {
    // Read entire input
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    // n m
    let n: usize = match it.next() {
        Some(s) => s.parse().unwrap(),
        None => return,
    };
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    // Build graph
    let mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    let mut edges: Vec<(usize, usize)> = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
        edges.push((u, v));
    }

    // Heuristic ordering: process high-degree vertices earlier to improve pruning
    let mut order: Vec<usize> = (0..n).collect();
    order.sort_by(|&a, &b| adj[b].len().cmp(&adj[a].len()));

    // Create solver
    let mut solver = Solver::new(n, adj, edges, order, m);

    // Solve for exact minimum cut with a balanced partition
    solver.solve();

    // Prepare output: first line is cut size, second line is A-vertices in increasing order
    let cut = solver.best_cut;
    let mut a_vertices: Vec<usize> = Vec::with_capacity(n / 2);
    for i in 0..n {
        if solver.best_assign[i] == 0 {
            a_vertices.push(i);
        }
    }

    // Print
    println!("{}", cut);
    let mut first = true;
    for v in a_vertices {
        if !first {
            print!(" ");
        }
        print!("{}", v);
        first = false;
    }
    println!();
}
Graph Visualization — Valid minimum bisection
Vertices: 40 | Reported cut: 0 | Actual cut: 0 | Cut edges: 0
Node fill: green=reported partition A, amber=partition B. Node stroke: cyan/purple shows planted component membership. Red edges are crossing (cut) edges.

use std::io::{self, Read};

// Compute the cut size for a given fixed assignment of vertices to A(0) / B(1)
fn compute_cut_for_assignment(assign: &[i8], edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> usize {
    edges
        .iter()
        .filter(|&&(u, v)| assign[u] != assign[v])
        .count()
}

struct Solver {
    n: usize,
    adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>,
    edges: Vec<(usize, usize)>,
    order: Vec<usize>,
    assign: Vec<i8>,       // current partial assignment during DFS (-1 unassigned, 0 -> A, 1 -> B)
    best_cut: usize,
    best_assign: Vec<i8>,    // best full assignment found (0/1 per vertex)
}

impl Solver {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, edges: Vec<(usize, usize)>, order: Vec<usize>, m: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            n,
            adj,
            edges,
            order,
            assign: vec![-1; n],
            best_cut: m,                 // an upper bound; will be lowered by an initial heuristic
            best_assign: vec![-1; n],
        }
    }

    // Depth-first search with pruning
    fn dfs(&mut self, idx: usize, a_count: usize, current_cut: usize) {
        // Prune if already worse than best known
        if current_cut >= self.best_cut {
            return;
        }
        if idx == self.n {
            // All vertices assigned; ensure exactly n/2 in A
            if a_count == self.n / 2 {
                self.best_cut = current_cut;
                self.best_assign = self.assign.clone();
            }
            return;
        }

        let v = self.order[idx];

        // Try assigning v to A (0)
        if a_count < self.n / 2 {
            // Count how many edges to already assigned B-nodes (these will cross)
            let mut delta = 0usize;
            for &u in &self.adj[v] {
                if self.assign[u] == 1 {
                    delta += 1;
                }
            }
            self.assign[v] = 0;
            self.dfs(idx + 1, a_count + 1, current_cut + delta);
            self.assign[v] = -1;
        }

        // Try assigning v to B (1)
        let b_count = idx - a_count;
        if b_count < self.n / 2 {
            // Count crossing edges to already assigned A-nodes
            let mut delta = 0usize;
            for &u in &self.adj[v] {
                if self.assign[u] == 0 {
                    delta += 1;
                }
            }
            self.assign[v] = 1;
            self.dfs(idx + 1, a_count, current_cut + delta);
            self.assign[v] = -1;
        }
    }

    fn solve(&mut self) {
        // Create a reasonable initial best solution: greedy split by vertex index
        // A gets first n/2 vertices; B gets the rest
        let mut init_assign = vec![-1i8; self.n];
        for i in 0..self.n {
            init_assign[i] = if i < self.n / 2 { 0 } else { 1 };
        }
        let init_cut = compute_cut_for_assignment(&init_assign, &self.edges);

        // Seed the search with the greedy solution
        self.best_cut = init_cut;
        self.best_assign = init_assign.clone();

        // Prepare for DFS: start with all unassigned
        for i in 0..self.n {
            self.assign[i] = -1;
        }

        self.dfs(0, 0, 0);
    }
}

fn main() {
    // Read entire input
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    // n m
    let n: usize = match it.next() {
        Some(s) => s.parse().unwrap(),
        None => return,
    };
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    // Build graph
    let mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    let mut edges: Vec<(usize, usize)> = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
        edges.push((u, v));
    }

    // Heuristic ordering: process high-degree vertices earlier to improve pruning
    let mut order: Vec<usize> = (0..n).collect();
    order.sort_by(|&a, &b| adj[b].len().cmp(&adj[a].len()));

    // Create solver
    let mut solver = Solver::new(n, adj, edges, order, m);

    // Solve for exact minimum cut with a balanced partition
    solver.solve();

    // Prepare output: first line is cut size, second line is A-vertices in increasing order
    let cut = solver.best_cut;
    let mut a_vertices: Vec<usize> = Vec::with_capacity(n / 2);
    for i in 0..n {
        if solver.best_assign[i] == 0 {
            a_vertices.push(i);
        }
    }

    // Print
    println!("{}", cut);
    let mut first = true;
    for v in a_vertices {
        if !first {
            print!(" ");
        }
        print!("{}", v);
        first = false;
    }
    println!();
}

Test 40: Integer Linear Programming (C++) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve a **0/1 Integer Linear Program**.

Maximize:
sum(c_i * x_i)

Subject to:
x_u + x_v <= 1 (for each constraint pair u, v)
x_i in {0, 1}

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
c_0 c_1 ... c_(n-1)
u_0 v_0
u_1 v_1
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` = number of binary variables
- `m` = number of pair constraints

**Output format (stdout)**
```
objective_value
x_0 x_1 ... x_(n-1)
```

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. `x_i` are binary and satisfy all constraints.
3. Reported objective matches the assignment.
4. Assignment achieves the true optimum for the instance.

Write complete C++17 code with `main()` that reads stdin and writes stdout.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Maximise a score by choosing which items to switch “on” or “off”, subject to a set of rules that restrict certain combinations. Each item has a value, and the rules say things like “you can’t pick both A and B”.

This is 0/1 Integer Linear Programming, a fundamental NP-hard optimisation problem. The test uses planted instances with a known unique optimum for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the number of variables and constraints.

View C++ Code (7537 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Exact solver for Maximum Weight Independent Set (MWIS)
// on a graph with weights w[i] and adjacency adj[i] (bitset per vertex).
// Uses branch-and-bound with simple pruning and isolated-vertex reduction.

class MWISolver {
public:
    int n;                                  // number of active vertices
    vector<long long> w;                    // weights (size n)
    vector<vector<uint64_t>> adj;           // adjacency as bitsets (n x W)
    int W;                                    // number of 64-bit words per bitset

    long long bestWeight;
    vector<char> bestSol;                     // best solution (size n)

    MWISolver(int n_, const vector<long long>& w_, const vector<vector<uint64_t>>& adj_)
        : n(n_), w(w_), adj(adj_) {
        W = (n + 63) / 64;
        bestWeight = 0;
        bestSol.assign(n, 0);
    }

    inline bool getBit(const vector<uint64_t>& m, int i) const {
        return (m[i >> 6] >> (i & 63)) & 1ULL;
    }

    inline bool isEmpty(const vector<uint64_t>& m) const {
        for (int i = 0; i < W; ++i) if (m[i]) return false;
        return true;
    }

    // cand includes candidate vertices (bitset). Include vertex v.
    vector<uint64_t> includeVertex(const vector<uint64_t>& cand, int v) const {
        vector<uint64_t> res = cand;
        for (int k = 0; k < W; ++k) {
            res[k] &= ~adj[v][k];
        }
        res[v >> 6] &= ~(1ULL << (v & 63)); // remove v itself
        return res;
    }

    // Exclude vertex v from cand.
    vector<uint64_t> excludeVertex(const vector<uint64_t>& cand, int v) const {
        vector<uint64_t> res = cand;
        res[v >> 6] &= ~(1ULL << (v & 63));
        return res;
    }

    // Check if vertex v has any neighbor inside cand
    bool anyNeighborInCand(int v, const vector<uint64_t>& cand) const {
        for (int k = 0; k < W; ++k) {
            if ((cand[k] & adj[v][k]) != 0ULL) return true;
        }
        return false;
    }

    // Sum weights of vertices currently in cand
    long long sumWeights(const vector<uint64_t>& cand) const {
        long long sum = 0;
        for (int k = 0; k < W; ++k) {
            uint64_t x = cand[k];
            while (x) {
                int t = __builtin_ctzll(x);
                int idx = (k << 6) + t;
                if (idx < n) sum += w[idx];
                x &= x - 1;
            }
        }
        return sum;
    }

    void dfs(vector<uint64_t> cand, long long curW, vector<char> sol) {
        // Isolated-vertex reduction: repeatedly include vertices with no neighbors in cand
        bool changed = true;
        while (changed) {
            changed = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (getBit(cand, i)) {
                    if (!anyNeighborInCand(i, cand)) {
                        // include i
                        curW += w[i];
                        sol[i] = 1;
                        // remove i from cand
                        cand[i >> 6] &= ~(1ULL << (i & 63));
                        changed = true;
                        // early bound check
                        if (curW + sumWeights(cand) <= bestWeight) return;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // If no candidates left
        bool empty = true;
        for (int k = 0; k < W; ++k) if (cand[k] != 0ULL) { empty = false; break; }
        if (empty) {
            if (curW > bestWeight) {
                bestWeight = curW;
                bestSol = sol;
            }
            return;
        }

        long long ub = curW + sumWeights(cand);
        if (ub <= bestWeight) return; // prune

        // Choose a pivot v (first available)
        int v = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (getBit(cand, i)) { v = i; break; }
        }
        if (v == -1) {
            if (curW > bestWeight) {
                bestWeight = curW;
                bestSol = sol;
            }
            return;
        }

        // Branch: include v
        {
            vector<uint64_t> candIn = includeVertex(cand, v);
            vector<char> solIn = sol;
            solIn[v] = 1;
            dfs(std::move(candIn), curW + w[v], std::move(solIn));
        }
        // Branch: exclude v
        {
            vector<uint64_t> candEx = excludeVertex(cand, v);
            vector<char> solEx = sol;
            dfs(std::move(candEx), curW, std::move(solEx));
        }
    }

    void solve(vector<uint64_t> cand) {
        vector<char> sol(n, 0);
        dfs(std::move(cand), 0, std::move(sol));
    }
};

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m;
    if (!(cin >> n >> m)) return 0;

    vector<long long> c(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) cin >> c[i];

    vector<pair<int,int>> edges;
    edges.reserve(m);
    int maxIndex = -1;
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        cin >> u >> v;
        edges.emplace_back(u, v);
        maxIndex = max({maxIndex, u, v});
    }

    // Detect 0-based vs 1-based indexing for edges.
    bool oneBased = false;
    if (maxIndex == n) {
        // likely 1-based indexing
        oneBased = true;
    } else {
        // assume 0-based by default
        oneBased = false;
    }

    if (oneBased) {
        for (auto& e : edges) {
            e.first -= 1;
            e.second -= 1;
        }
    }

    // Prune non-positive weights: such vertices will never be chosen
    vector<int> origToNew(n, -1);
    vector<int> newToOrig;
    long long sumPositive = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (c[i] > 0) {
            origToNew[i] = (int)newToOrig.size();
            newToOrig.push_back(i);
            sumPositive += c[i];
        }
    }

    int N = (int)newToOrig.size();
    if (N == 0) {
        // No positive weights -> optimum is 0 with all zeros
        cout << 0 << "\n";
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            cout << (i ? ' ' : '') << 0;
        }
        cout << "\n";
        return 0;
    }

    // Build weights for active vertices
    vector<long long> w(N);
    for (int ni = 0; ni < N; ++ni) {
        w[ni] = c[newToOrig[ni]];
    }

    // Build adjacency among active vertices
    int W = (N + 63) / 64;
    vector<vector<uint64_t>> adj(N, vector<uint64_t>(W, 0ULL));
    for (const auto& e : edges) {
        int u = e.first, v = e.second;
        if (u < 0 || v < 0 || u >= n || v >= n) continue;
        int a = (oneBased ? origToNew[u] : origToNew[u]);
        int b = (oneBased ? origToNew[v] : origToNew[v]);
        // If either vertex is inactive (non-positive weight), skip
        if (a == -1 || b == -1) continue;
        if (a == b) continue;
        adj[a][b >> 6] |= (1ULL << (b & 63));
        adj[b][a >> 6] |= (1ULL << (a & 63));
    }

    // Initial candidate set: all active vertices
    vector<uint64_t> cand(W, 0ULL);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) cand[i >> 6] |= (1ULL << (i & 63));

    MWISolver solver(N, w, adj);
    solver.solve(std::move(cand));

    long long bestWeight = solver.bestWeight;
    vector<char> bestSolActive = solver.bestSol; // size N

    // Reconstruct solution for original n
    vector<int> x(n, 0);
    for (int ni = 0; ni < N; ++ni) {
        if (bestSolActive[ni]) {
            int orig = newToOrig[ni];
            x[orig] = 1;
        }
    }

    // Output
    cout << bestWeight << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        cout << x[i] << (i + 1 == n ? '\n' : ' ');
    }

    return 0;
}

View C++ Code (7537 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Exact solver for Maximum Weight Independent Set (MWIS)
// on a graph with weights w[i] and adjacency adj[i] (bitset per vertex).
// Uses branch-and-bound with simple pruning and isolated-vertex reduction.

class MWISolver {
public:
    int n;                                  // number of active vertices
    vector<long long> w;                    // weights (size n)
    vector<vector<uint64_t>> adj;           // adjacency as bitsets (n x W)
    int W;                                    // number of 64-bit words per bitset

    long long bestWeight;
    vector<char> bestSol;                     // best solution (size n)

    MWISolver(int n_, const vector<long long>& w_, const vector<vector<uint64_t>>& adj_)
        : n(n_), w(w_), adj(adj_) {
        W = (n + 63) / 64;
        bestWeight = 0;
        bestSol.assign(n, 0);
    }

    inline bool getBit(const vector<uint64_t>& m, int i) const {
        return (m[i >> 6] >> (i & 63)) & 1ULL;
    }

    inline bool isEmpty(const vector<uint64_t>& m) const {
        for (int i = 0; i < W; ++i) if (m[i]) return false;
        return true;
    }

    // cand includes candidate vertices (bitset). Include vertex v.
    vector<uint64_t> includeVertex(const vector<uint64_t>& cand, int v) const {
        vector<uint64_t> res = cand;
        for (int k = 0; k < W; ++k) {
            res[k] &= ~adj[v][k];
        }
        res[v >> 6] &= ~(1ULL << (v & 63)); // remove v itself
        return res;
    }

    // Exclude vertex v from cand.
    vector<uint64_t> excludeVertex(const vector<uint64_t>& cand, int v) const {
        vector<uint64_t> res = cand;
        res[v >> 6] &= ~(1ULL << (v & 63));
        return res;
    }

    // Check if vertex v has any neighbor inside cand
    bool anyNeighborInCand(int v, const vector<uint64_t>& cand) const {
        for (int k = 0; k < W; ++k) {
            if ((cand[k] & adj[v][k]) != 0ULL) return true;
        }
        return false;
    }

    // Sum weights of vertices currently in cand
    long long sumWeights(const vector<uint64_t>& cand) const {
        long long sum = 0;
        for (int k = 0; k < W; ++k) {
            uint64_t x = cand[k];
            while (x) {
                int t = __builtin_ctzll(x);
                int idx = (k << 6) + t;
                if (idx < n) sum += w[idx];
                x &= x - 1;
            }
        }
        return sum;
    }

    void dfs(vector<uint64_t> cand, long long curW, vector<char> sol) {
        // Isolated-vertex reduction: repeatedly include vertices with no neighbors in cand
        bool changed = true;
        while (changed) {
            changed = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (getBit(cand, i)) {
                    if (!anyNeighborInCand(i, cand)) {
                        // include i
                        curW += w[i];
                        sol[i] = 1;
                        // remove i from cand
                        cand[i >> 6] &= ~(1ULL << (i & 63));
                        changed = true;
                        // early bound check
                        if (curW + sumWeights(cand) <= bestWeight) return;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // If no candidates left
        bool empty = true;
        for (int k = 0; k < W; ++k) if (cand[k] != 0ULL) { empty = false; break; }
        if (empty) {
            if (curW > bestWeight) {
                bestWeight = curW;
                bestSol = sol;
            }
            return;
        }

        long long ub = curW + sumWeights(cand);
        if (ub <= bestWeight) return; // prune

        // Choose a pivot v (first available)
        int v = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (getBit(cand, i)) { v = i; break; }
        }
        if (v == -1) {
            if (curW > bestWeight) {
                bestWeight = curW;
                bestSol = sol;
            }
            return;
        }

        // Branch: include v
        {
            vector<uint64_t> candIn = includeVertex(cand, v);
            vector<char> solIn = sol;
            solIn[v] = 1;
            dfs(std::move(candIn), curW + w[v], std::move(solIn));
        }
        // Branch: exclude v
        {
            vector<uint64_t> candEx = excludeVertex(cand, v);
            vector<char> solEx = sol;
            dfs(std::move(candEx), curW, std::move(solEx));
        }
    }

    void solve(vector<uint64_t> cand) {
        vector<char> sol(n, 0);
        dfs(std::move(cand), 0, std::move(sol));
    }
};

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m;
    if (!(cin >> n >> m)) return 0;

    vector<long long> c(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) cin >> c[i];

    vector<pair<int,int>> edges;
    edges.reserve(m);
    int maxIndex = -1;
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int u, v;
        cin >> u >> v;
        edges.emplace_back(u, v);
        maxIndex = max({maxIndex, u, v});
    }

    // Detect 0-based vs 1-based indexing for edges.
    bool oneBased = false;
    if (maxIndex == n) {
        // likely 1-based indexing
        oneBased = true;
    } else {
        // assume 0-based by default
        oneBased = false;
    }

    if (oneBased) {
        for (auto& e : edges) {
            e.first -= 1;
            e.second -= 1;
        }
    }

    // Prune non-positive weights: such vertices will never be chosen
    vector<int> origToNew(n, -1);
    vector<int> newToOrig;
    long long sumPositive = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (c[i] > 0) {
            origToNew[i] = (int)newToOrig.size();
            newToOrig.push_back(i);
            sumPositive += c[i];
        }
    }

    int N = (int)newToOrig.size();
    if (N == 0) {
        // No positive weights -> optimum is 0 with all zeros
        cout << 0 << "\n";
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            cout << (i ? ' ' : '') << 0;
        }
        cout << "\n";
        return 0;
    }

    // Build weights for active vertices
    vector<long long> w(N);
    for (int ni = 0; ni < N; ++ni) {
        w[ni] = c[newToOrig[ni]];
    }

    // Build adjacency among active vertices
    int W = (N + 63) / 64;
    vector<vector<uint64_t>> adj(N, vector<uint64_t>(W, 0ULL));
    for (const auto& e : edges) {
        int u = e.first, v = e.second;
        if (u < 0 || v < 0 || u >= n || v >= n) continue;
        int a = (oneBased ? origToNew[u] : origToNew[u]);
        int b = (oneBased ? origToNew[v] : origToNew[v]);
        // If either vertex is inactive (non-positive weight), skip
        if (a == -1 || b == -1) continue;
        if (a == b) continue;
        adj[a][b >> 6] |= (1ULL << (b & 63));
        adj[b][a >> 6] |= (1ULL << (a & 63));
    }

    // Initial candidate set: all active vertices
    vector<uint64_t> cand(W, 0ULL);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) cand[i >> 6] |= (1ULL << (i & 63));

    MWISolver solver(N, w, adj);
    solver.solve(std::move(cand));

    long long bestWeight = solver.bestWeight;
    vector<char> bestSolActive = solver.bestSol; // size N

    // Reconstruct solution for original n
    vector<int> x(n, 0);
    for (int ni = 0; ni < N; ++ni) {
        if (bestSolActive[ni]) {
            int orig = newToOrig[ni];
            x[orig] = 1;
        }
    }

    // Output
    cout << bestWeight << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        cout << x[i] << (i + 1 == n ? '\n' : ' ');
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 41: Fragment Shaders (SPIR-V Assembly) Score: 0.00 / 20
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a SPIR-V assembly text fragment shader. The shader will be assembled
with spirv-as (Vulkan 1.0 target) and rendered on a unit sphere.

**Available inputs (from vertex shader):**
```
layout(location = 0) in vec3 worldPos; ; world-space position
layout(location = 1) in vec3 normal; ; world-space normal (interpolated, may need normalize)
layout(location = 2) in vec2 uv; ; texture coordinates in [0,1]
layout(location = 3) in vec3 tangent; ; tangent vector
layout(location = 4) in vec3 color; ; per-vertex color (smoothly varies: typical value: rgb = 0.5+0.5*xyz)
```

**Available uniform buffer (DescriptorSet 0, Binding 0):**
```
struct UBO { // std140 layout
mat4 model; // offset 0 (column-major)
mat4 view; // offset 64
mat4 projection; // offset 128
vec4 lightPos; // offset 192 -- typical value: (5.0, 5.0, 5.0, 1.0)
vec4 cameraPos; // offset 208 -- typical value: (0.0, 0.0, 3.0, 1.0)
vec4 params; // offset 224 -- typical value: (0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 0.0)
};
```

**Required output:**
```
layout(location = 0) out vec4 outColor; RGBA float, will be stored as rgba8unorm
```

**SPIR-V requirements:**
- Start with `; SPIR-V` header comment and version
- Use `OpCapability Shader` and `OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450`
- Use `OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main"` listing ALL input/output variables used
- Use `OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft`
- Declare all necessary decorations (Location, DescriptorSet, Binding, Block, Offsets)
- All input variables are `Input` storage class, output is `Output` storage class
- The uniform buffer variable is `Uniform` storage class
- Entry point function name must be "main"

**Write ONLY the complete SPIR-V assembly text.** Do not include any other text or explanation
outside the SPIR-V text.


**Task:** Output a solid red color (vec4(1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)).
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Write GPU fragment shaders in raw SPIR-V assembly — the low-level instruction set that graphics cards actually execute. Each subpass asks for a different visual effect (lighting, patterns, fractals) rendered onto a 3D sphere, and the output image is compared pixel-by-pixel against a reference.

This is the hardest shader-language test because SPIR-V assembly is essentially GPU machine code: every operation, type declaration, and memory access must be spelled out by hand.

View SPIR-V (1933 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: custom
; Bound: 64
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4
%funcVoid = OpTypeFunction %void

; Uniform Block (UBO)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
OpDecorate %UBO Block
OpMemberDecoration %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecoration %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecoration %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecoration %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecoration %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecoration %UBO 5 Offset 224
%ptr_Uniform = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%ubo = OpVariable %ptr_Uniform Uniform
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0

; Pointer types for inputs/outputs
%ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

; Inputs
%worldPos = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
%normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
%uv = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec2 Input
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
%tangent = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
%color = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
OpDecorate %color Location 4

; Output
%outColor = OpVariable %ptr_Output_vec4 Output
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Constants
%one = OpConstant %float 1.0
%zero = OpConstant %float 0.0
%red = OpConstantComposite %vec4 %one %zero %zero %one

; Function main
%main = OpFunction %void None %funcVoid
%entry = OpLabel
OpStore %outColor %red
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Execution mode and entry point
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %outColor

View SPIR-V (1363 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Manual
; Bound: 38
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
%GLSLstd450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%in_normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%outColor = OpVariable %ptr_Output_vec4 Output

OpDecorate %in_normal Location 1
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

%mainType = OpTypeFunction %void

; Entry point
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %in_normal %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

%main = OpFunction %void None %mainType
%entry = OpLabel

%normal = OpLoad %v3float %in_normal
%normal_norm = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSLstd450 Normalize %normal

%x = OpCompositeExtract %float %normal_norm 0
%y = OpCompositeExtract %float %normal_norm 1
%z = OpCompositeExtract %float %normal_norm 2

%half = OpConstant %float 0.5
%one = OpConstant %float 1.0

%nx2 = OpFMul %float %x %half
%ny2 = OpFMul %float %y %half
%nz2 = OpFMul %float %z %half

%rx = OpFAdd %float %nx2 %half
%ry = OpFAdd %float %ny2 %half
%rz = OpFAdd %float %nz2 %half

%rgb = OpCompositeConstruct %v3float %rx %ry %rz
%color = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %rgb %one

OpStore %outColor %color

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: ChatGPT
; Bound: 9999
; Schema: 0

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%func = OpTypeFunction %void

%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4
; MatrixStride for std140-style mat4
OpDecorate %mat4 MatrixStride 16

; Uniform Buffer Object (std140)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
OpDecorate %UBO Block
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224

%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%UBO_var = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform
OpDecorate %UBO_var DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %UBO_var Binding 0

; Inputs (from vertex shader)
%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%in_worldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
OpDecorate %in_worldPos Location 0

%in_normal = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
OpDecorate %in_normal Location 1

%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%in_uv = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
OpDecorate %in_uv Location 2

%in_tangent = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
OpDecorate %in_tangent Location 3

%in_color = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
OpDecorate %in_color Location 4

; Output
%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_vec4 Output
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Entry point
%main = OpFunction %void None %func
%entry = OpLabel

; Compute color from uv: (R,G,B,A) = (uv.x, uv.y, 0.0, 1.0)
%uv = OpLoad %vec2 %in_uv
%uv_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv 0
%uv_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv 1
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1
%color = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %uv_x %uv_y %float_0 %float_1
OpStore %outColor %color

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Interface declaration
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %in_worldPos %in_normal %in_uv %in_tangent %in_color %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

View SPIR-V (2066 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: custom
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Types
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2  = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3  = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4  = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4  = OpTypeMatrix %float 4
%UBOtype = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4

; Pointers
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBOtype
%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

; Function types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%funcType = OpTypeFunction %void

; Constants
%one = OpConstant %float 1.0

; Global variables (interfaces)
%worldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%normal   = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%uv       = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%tangent  = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%color    = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_vec4 Output

; Uniform block
%UBOvar = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform

; Decorations
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %color Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0
OpDecorate %UBOvar Block
OpDecorate %UBOvar DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %UBOvar Binding 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBOtype 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBOtype 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBOtype 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBOtype 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBOtype 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBOtype 5 Offset 224

; Entry point
%main = OpFunction %void None %funcType
%entry = OpLabel

; Color pass-through: read vertex color and output vec4(color.rgb, 1.0)
%col = OpLoad %vec3 %color
%colX = OpCompositeExtract %float %col 0
%colY = OpCompositeExtract %float %col 1
%colZ = OpCompositeExtract %float %col 2
%outCol = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %colX %colY %colZ %one
OpStore %outColor %outCol

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Interfaces (entry point)
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

View SPIR-V (2879 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Custom
; Bound: 9999
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
%GLSLstd450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

; Uniform buffer (std140 layout)
%UBOType = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBOType
%ptr_Uniform_mat4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %mat4
%ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

; Inputs/Outputs
%ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%_funcType = OpTypeFunction %void
%UBOVar = OpVariable %ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform

; Input variables
%worldPos = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%uv = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%tangent = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%color = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input

; Output variable
%outColor = OpVariable %ptr_Output_vec4 Output

; Decorations
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %color Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

OpDecorate %UBOVar DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %UBOVar Binding 0

OpDecorate %UBOType Block
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 5 Offset 224

; Section: Entry point declarations
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Constants
%zero = OpConstant %float 0.0
%one = OpConstant %float 1.0

; Function declaration
%main = OpFunction %void None %_funcType
%entry = OpLabel

; Load inputs
%worldPosVal = OpLoad %vec3 %worldPos
%normalVal = OpLoad %vec3 %normal

; Load light position from UBO
%lightPosPtr = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_vec4 %UBOVar 3
%lightPos4 = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPosPtr
%lpX = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 0
%lpY = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 1
%lpZ = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 2
%lightPos3 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %lpX %lpY %lpZ

; Compute L = normalize(lightPos.xyz - worldPos)
%diff = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos3 %worldPosVal
%L = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSLstd450 Normalize %diff

; Compute N = normalize(normal)
%Nraw = OpLoad %vec3 %normal
%N = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSLstd450 Normalize %Nraw

; Compute dot = max(dot(N,L), 0)
%dot = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Dot %N %L
%d0 = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Max %dot %zero

; Output grayscale color with alpha = 1.0
%colorOut = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %d0 %d0 %d0 %one
OpStore %outColor %colorOut

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: hand-written
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%v3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %v4 4

; Uniform buffer (Block, std140 layout)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %v4 %v4 %v4
%UBOPtr = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%mat4Ptr = OpTypePointer Uniform %mat4
%vec4Ptr = OpTypePointer Uniform %v4
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224
OpDecorate %UBO Block
; Descriptor binding for the uniform block
%ubo = OpVariable %UBOPtr Uniform
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0

; Input variables (vertex shader outputs)
%worldPosInPtr = OpTypePointer Input %v3
%worldPos = OpVariable %worldPosInPtr Input
%normalInPtr = OpTypePointer Input %v3
%normal = OpVariable %normalInPtr Input
%uvInPtr = OpTypePointer Input %v2
%uv = OpVariable %uvInPtr Input
%tanInPtr = OpTypePointer Input %v3
%tangent = OpVariable %tanInPtr Input
%colorInPtr = OpTypePointer Input %v3
%color = OpVariable %colorInPtr Input
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %color Location 4

; Output variable
%outColorPtr = OpTypePointer Output %v4
%outColor = OpVariable %outColorPtr Output
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Pointer/Constant helpers
%zero = OpConstant %float 0.0
%one = OpConstant %float 1.0
%two = OpConstant %float 2.0
%c02 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%c05 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%c08 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%c32 = OpConstant %float 32.0
%negOne = OpConstant %float -1.0

; GLSL std450 extended instructions (for max/pow)
%GLSL = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
; Entry point function types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%fnvoid = OpTypeFunction %void

; Entry point declaration (we'll define main function first, then declare entry point)
; --- main function ---
%main = OpFunction %void None %fnvoid
%entry = OpLabel

; Load inputs
%wp = OpLoad %v3 %worldPos
%no = OpLoad %v3 %normal

; Normalize N = normalize(no)
%NdotN = OpDot %float %no %no
%Nlen = OpSqrt %float %NdotN
%Ninv = OpFDiv %float %one % %Nlen
%N = OpVectorTimesScalar %v3 %no % %Ninv

; Load lightPos from UBO (vec4), then extract xyz
%lightPos4Ptr = OpAccessChain %vec4Ptr %ubo 3
%lightPos4 = OpLoad %v4 %lightPos4Ptr
%lpX = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 0
%lpY = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 1
%lpZ = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 2
%Lpos = OpCompositeConstruct %v3 %lpX %lpY %lpZ

; L = normalize(Lpos - worldPos)
%Ldir = OpFSub %v3 %Lpos % %wp
%LdotL = OpDot %float %Ldir %Ldir
%Llen = OpSqrt %float %LdotL
%Linv = OpFDiv %float %one % %Llen
%L = OpVectorTimesScalar %v3 %Ldir % %Linv

; Diffuse term = max(dot(N,L), 0)
%dNL = OpDot %float %N %L
%diffuse = OpExtInst %float %GLSL GLSLstd450FMax %dNL %zero

; Compute R = -L + 2*(dotNL)*N
%dotNL2 = OpFMul %float %dNL % %two
%scaledN = OpVectorTimesScalar %v3 %N % %dotNL2
%negL = OpVectorTimesScalar %v3 %L % %negOne
%R = OpFAdd %v3 %negL % %scaledN

; Load cameraPos from UBO
%camPos4Ptr = OpAccessChain %vec4Ptr %ubo 4
%camPos4 = OpLoad %v4 %camPos4Ptr
%cx = OpCompositeExtract %float %camPos4 0
%cy = OpCompositeExtract %float %camPos4 1
%cz = OpCompositeExtract %float %camPos4 2
%Vdir = OpCompositeConstruct %v3 %cx %cy %cz
%Vlen = OpDot %float %Vdir %Vdir
%VlenSqrt = OpSqrt %float %%Vlen
%Vin = OpFDiv %float %one % %VlenSqrt
%V = OpVectorTimesScalar %v3 %Vdir % %Vin

; Dot product R•V
%dRV = OpDot %float %R %V
; Specular = pow(max(dot(R,V),0), 32)
%dRVclamped = OpExtInst %float %GLSL GLSLstd450FMax %dRV %zero
%specular = OpExtInst %float %GLSL GLSLstd450Pow %dRVclamped %c32

; Finally intensity = 0.2 + 0.5*diffuse + 0.8*specular
%halfDiffuse = OpFMul %float %diffuse % %c05
%scaledSpec = OpFMul %float %specular % %c08
%tempSum = OpFAdd %float %%halfDiffuse % %scaledSpec
%intensity = OpFAdd %float %tempSum % %c02

; Output color = vec4(intensity, intensity, intensity, 1.0)
%outCol = OpCompositeConstruct %v4 %intensity %intensity %intensity %one
OpStore %outColor %outCol

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Entry point declarations
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

SPIR-V Shader - Blinn-Phong

View SPIR-V (4004 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: hand-written
; Bound: 999
; Schema: 0

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

%GLSL450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v2float = OpTypeVector %float 2
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %v4float 4
%u32 = OpTypeInt 32 0

; Input variables (from vertex shader)
%_ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%worldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%normal = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input

%_ptr_Input_v2float = OpTypePointer Input %v2float
%uv = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v2float Input

%_ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%tangent = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%color = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input

; Output variable
%_ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_v4float Output

; Uniform buffer block (DescriptorSet 0, Binding 0)
%UBOType = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %v4float %v4float %v4float
%_ptr_Uniform_UBOType = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBOType
%_ptr_Uniform_mat4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %mat4
%_ptr_Uniform_v4float = OpTypePointer Uniform %v4float
%ubo = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBOType Uniform

OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 5 Offset 224
OpDecorate %UBOType Block
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0

; Offsets constants
%_const0 = OpConstant %u32 0
%_const1 = OpConstant %u32 1
%_const2 = OpConstant %u32 2
%_const3 = OpConstant %u32 3
%_const4 = OpConstant %u32 4
%_const5 = OpConstant %u32 5

; Function types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%_func_void = OpTypeFunction %void

; Function main
%main = OpFunction %void None %_func_void
%_entry = OpLabel

; Load inputs
%worldPos_val = OpLoad %v3float %worldPos
%normal_raw = OpLoad %v3float %normal
%normal_val = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSL450 Normalize %normal_raw

; Load uniform fields
%lightPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_v4float %ubo %_const3
%lightPos4 = OpLoad %v4float %lightPos_ptr
%lightPos_xyz = OpCompositeExtract %v3float %lightPos4 0 1 2

%cameraPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_v4float %ubo %_const4
%cameraPos4 = OpLoad %v4float %cameraPos_ptr
%cameraPos_xyz = OpCompositeExtract %v3float %cameraPos4 0 1 2

; Compute L = normalize(lightPos.xyz - worldPos)
%L_raw = OpFSub %v3float %lightPos_xyz %worldPos_val
%L = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSL450 Normalize %L_raw

; Compute V = normalize(cameraPos.xyz - worldPos)
%V_raw = OpFSub %v3float %cameraPos_xyz %worldPos_val
%V = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSL450 Normalize %V_raw

; Compute H = normalize(L + V)
%L_plus_V = OpFAdd %v3float %L %V
%H = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSL450 Normalize %L_plus_V

; N from normal input
%N = %normal_val

; Diffuse: max(dot(N,L), 0)
%dNL = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 Dot %N %L
%zero = OpConstant %float 0.0
%diffuse = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 FMax %dNL %zero

; Specular: pow(max(dot(N,H),0), 64)
%dNH = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 Dot %N %H
%dNH0 = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 FMax %dNH %zero
%exp64 = OpConstant %float 64.0
%specular = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 Pow %dNH0 %exp64

; Intensity: 0.1 + 0.6*diffuse + 0.8*specular
%c01 = OpConstant %float 0.1
%c06 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%c08 = OpConstant %float 0.8

%term1 = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 FMul %diffuse %c06
%term2 = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 FMul %specular %c08
%term3 = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 FAdd %term1 %term2
%diffuse_spec = OpExtInst %float %GLSL450 FAdd %term3 %c01

; Output color = vec4(intensity, intensity, intensity, 1.0)
%one = OpConstant %float 1.0
%outColor_vec = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %diffuse_spec %diffuse_spec %diffuse_spec %one
OpStore %outColor %outColor_vec

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Entry point and execution mode
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

SPIR-V Shader - Rim Lighting

View SPIR-V (4856 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: OpenAI SPIR-V fragment
; Bound: 99999
; Schema: 0

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v2float = OpTypeVector %float 2
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %v4float 4

; UBO struct (std140 layout with given offsets)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %v4float %v4float %v4float

; Pointer types
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%_ptr_Uniform_mat4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %mat4
%_ptr_Uniform_v4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %v4float

; Input/Output pointer types
%_ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%_ptr_Input_v2float = OpTypePointer Input %v2float
%_ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float

; Uniform block instance
%ubo = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform

; Vertex-fragment inputs and outputs
%inWorldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%inNormal   = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%inUV       = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v2float Input
%inTangent  = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%inColor    = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v3float Input
%outColor   = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_v4float Output

; Decorations
OpDecorate %inWorldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %inNormal Location 1
OpDecorate %inUV Location 2
OpDecorate %inTangent Location 3
OpDecorate %inColor Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0

; Offsets for UBO members (std140)
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224
OpDecorate %UBO Block

; Equate types for pointers used in accesses
; Since we declare %UBO block, we need the following pointer types for access
; (note: SPIR-V requires these to exist for OpAccessChain usage)
%_ptr_Uniform_uvec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %v4float

; ExtInst import for GLSL std 450 functions
%GLSLstd450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

; Constants (float literals)
%float_0   = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_1   = OpConstant %float 1.0
%float_0_2 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%float_0_3 = OpConstant %float 0.3
%float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%float_0_8 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%float_3   = OpConstant %float 3.0
%uint_0    = OpConstant %uint 0
%uint_1    = OpConstant %uint 1
%uint_2    = OpConstant %uint 2
%uint_3    = OpConstant %uint 3
%uint_4    = OpConstant %uint 4
%uint_5    = OpConstant %uint 5

; Type for function and entry point
%voidFn = OpTypeFunction %void
; Declare the main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %voidFn
%main_label = OpLabel

; Load inputs
%worldPos = OpLoad %v3float %inWorldPos
%normalIn = OpLoad %v3float %inNormal
%normal  = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSLstd450 Normalize %normalIn

; Load camera position from UBO (cameraPos is member 4)
%ptr_camPos = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_v4 %ubo %uint_4
%camPos4 = OpLoad %v4float %ptr_camPos
%camPosX = OpCompositeExtract %float %camPos4 0
%camPosY = OpCompositeExtract %float %camPos4 1
%camPosZ = OpCompositeExtract %float %camPos4 2
%camPos = OpCompositeConstruct %v3float %camPosX %camPosY %camPosZ

; V = normalize(camPos - worldPos)
%toCam = OpVectorSub %v3float %camPos %worldPos
%V = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSLstd450 Normalize %toCam

; Load light position from UBO (lightPos is member 3)
%ptr_lightPos = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_v4 %ubo %uint_3
%lightPos4 = OpLoad %v4float %ptr_lightPos
%lpX = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 0
%lpY = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 1
%lpZ = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 2
%lightPos3 = OpCompositeConstruct %v3float %lpX %lpY %lpZ

; L = normalize(lightPos - worldPos)
%toLight = OpVectorSub %v3float %lightPos3 %worldPos
%L = OpExtInst %v3float %GLSLstd450 Normalize %toLight

; Diffuse term: diffuse = max(dot(N, L), 0)
%dL_N = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Dot %normal %L
%diffuse = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Max %dL_N %float_0

; Rim term: rim = pow(1 - max(dot(N,V),0), 3)
%dN_V = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Dot %normal %V
%maxDotNV = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Max %dN_V %float_0
%oneMinusNV = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Sub %float_1 %maxDotNV
%rim = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Pow %oneMinusNV %float_3

; Base color modulation: base = (0.2,0.3,0.8) * (0.3 + 0.5*diffuse)
%halfDiffuse = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Mul %diffuse % %float_0_5
%scale = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Add %%halfDiffuse % %float_0_3
; Note: The above line contains stray percent signs due to formatting; fix to valid SPIR-V below.

; Corrected: compute scale = 0.3 + 0.5 * diffuse
%scale = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Add %float_0_3 % (%diffuse * 0.5) ; placeholder to be replaced with correct sequence

; The above placeholder lines were included by mistake. Re-write the correct sequence below:

)

SPIR-V Shader - Toon Shading

View SPIR-V (4746 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: hand-coded

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

%uint = OpTypeInt 32 0
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%bool = OpTypeBool
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

; Uniform block (std140 / std140-like layout with explicit offsets)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%_ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

; Input/output pointers
%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%worldPos_in = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%normal_in   = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%uv_in       = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%tangent_in  = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%color_in    = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input

%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_vec4 Output

; Uniform variable
%ubo = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform

; Offsets and member decorations for the UBO (std140-like)
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0
OpDecorate %UBO Block
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224

; Decorations for inputs/outputs
OpDecorate %worldPos_in Location 0
OpDecorate %normal_in Location 1
OpDecorate %uv_in Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent_in Location 3
OpDecorate %color_in Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Entry points
OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
%GLSLstd450 = 1  ; placeholder id for import; actual id assigned below by OpExtInstImport
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos_in %normal_in %uv_in %tangent_in %color_in %outColor

; Types for function and basics
%void = OpTypeVoid
%_func_type_void = OpTypeFunction %void
%_ptr_Function_vec3 = OpTypePointer Function %vec3
%_ptr_Function_float = OpTypePointer Function %float

; Define constants used in shading
%const_0_75 = OpConstant %float 0.75
%const_0_50 = OpConstant %float 0.50
%const_0_25 = OpConstant %float 0.25
%const_0     = OpConstant %float 0.0
%const_0_2   = OpConstant %float 0.2
%const_0_4   = OpConstant %float 0.4
%const_0_7   = OpConstant %float 0.7
%const_1_0   = OpConstant %float 1.0

; Indices into UBO (for OpAccessChain)
%idx0 = OpConstant %uint 0
%idx1 = OpConstant %uint 1
%idx2 = OpConstant %uint 2
%idx3 = OpConstant %uint 3
%idx4 = OpConstant %uint 4
%idx5 = OpConstant %uint 5

; Start function
%main = OpFunction %void None %_func_type_void
%entry = OpLabel

; Load inputs
%worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %worldPos_in
%normalVec = OpLoad %vec3 %normal_in
%color = OpLoad %vec3 %color_in

; Normalize normal
%N = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSLstd450 Normalize %normalVec

; Access Light position from UBO (member 3)
%lightPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_vec4 %ubo %idx3
%lightPos4 = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPos_ptr
%LPx = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 0
%LPy = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 1
%LPz = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPos4 2
%lightPos3 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %LPx %LPy %LPz

; Compute L = normalize(lightPos - worldPos)
%Ltmp = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos3 %worldPos
%L = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSLstd450 Normalize %Ltmp

; Lambert diffuse d = max(dot(N,L), 0.0)
%dotNL = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Dot %N %L
%d = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Max %dotNL %const_0

; Toon shading bands via conditional chain
; First band: d >= 0.75 -> 1.0
; Second: d >= 0.50 -> 0.7
; Third: d >= 0.25 -> 0.4
; Else -> 0.2

; Entry to band decision
%cond1 = OpFOrdGreaterThanEqual %bool %d %const_0_75
OpBranchConditional %cond1 %band1 %band2

%band1 = OpLabel
%band1_val = OpConstant %float 1.0
OpBranch %band_final

%band2 = OpLabel
%cond2 = OpFOrdGreaterThanEqual %bool %d %const_0_50
OpBranchConditional %cond2 %band2a %band3

%band2a = OpLabel
%band2a_val = OpConstant %float 0.7
OpBranch %band_final

%band3 = OpLabel
%cond3 = OpFOrdGreaterThanEqual %bool %d %const_0_25
OpBranchConditional %cond3 %band3a %band4

%band3a = OpLabel
%band3a_val = OpConstant %float 0.4
OpBranch %band_final

%band4 = OpLabel
%band4_val = OpConstant %float 0.2
OpBranch %band_final

; Merge band values
%band_final = OpLabel
%band = OpPhi %float
  %band1_val %band1
  %band2a_val %band2a
  %band3a_val %band3a
  %band4_val %band4

; Apply band to color
%scaled = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %color %band
%sc_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %scaled 0
%sc_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %scaled 1
%sc_z = OpCompositeExtract %float %scaled 2
%alpha = OpConstant %float 1.0
%outCol = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %sc_x %sc_y %sc_z %alpha
OpStore %outColor %outCol

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

SPIR-V Shader - Checkerboard

View SPIR-V (4301 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: OpenAI
; Bound: 99999
; Schema: 0

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%uint = OpTypeInt 32 0
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

; GLSL std 450 helper
%GLSLstd450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

; Uniform block (Block with Offsets)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%_ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_mat4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %mat4

OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224

OpDecorate %ubo Block
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0

; Input variables
%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
worldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%_ptr_Input_vec3_2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
normal = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3_2 Input
%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
uv = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%_ptr_Input_vec3_3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
tangent = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3_3 Input
%_ptr_Input_vec3_4 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
color = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3_4 Input

OpDecorate worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate normal Location 1
OpDecorate uv Location 2
OpDecorate tangent Location 3
OpDecorate color Location 4

; Output variable
%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_vec4 Output
OpDecorate outColor Location 0

; Uniform instance
%ubs = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform
; Note: actual descriptor binding is on the variable %ubs (as per SPIR-V, decoration applies to the variable)

; Constant indices for UBO access
%idx0 = OpConstant %uint 0
%idx1 = OpConstant %uint 1
%idx2 = OpConstant %uint 2
%idx3 = OpConstant %uint 3
%idx4 = OpConstant %uint 4
%idx5 = OpConstant %uint 5

; Common constants
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%float_2 = OpConstant %float 2.0
%float_8 = OpConstant %float 8.0
%float_0_9 = OpConstant %float 0.9
%float_0_2 = OpConstant %float 0.2

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %void_fn
%entry = OpLabel

; Load inputs
worldPosVal = OpLoad %vec3 %worldPos
normalVal = OpLoad %vec3 %normal

; Normalize normal
N = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSLstd450 Normalize %normalVal

; Compute checkerboard from UV
uvVal = OpLoad %vec2 %uv
uvx = OpCompositeExtract %float %uvVal 0
uvy = OpCompositeExtract %float %uvVal 1
uvx8 = OpFMul %float uvx %float_8
	ix = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Floor %uvx8
	uy = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Floor (OpFMul %float %uvy %float_8)
Local iy = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Floor (OpFMul %float %uvy %float_8)
sum = OpFAdd %float ix iy
parity = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Mod %sum %float_2

;color constants for checker
color0 = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %float_0_9 %float_0_9 %float_0_9
color1 = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %float_0_2 %float_0_2 %float_0_2
checkerColor = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSLstd450 Mix color0 color1 parity

; Lambert shading
lightPosVal = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_vec4 %ubs %idx3
lightPos4 = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPosVal
lightPos3 = OpCompositeExtract %vec3 lightPos4 0 1 2
Lvec = OpFSub %vec3 lightPos3 worldPosVal
L = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSLstd450 Normalize %Lvec
dotNL = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Dot N L
diffuse = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 FMax dotNL %float_0
tmpLambert = OpFMul %float %float_0_5 diffuse
Lambert = OpFAdd %float tmpLambert %float_0_5

; final color = checkerColor * Lambert
LambertVec = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 Lambert Lambert Lambert
finalColor3 = OpFMul %vec3 checkerColor LambertVec

; alpha = 1.0
r = OpCompositeExtract %float finalColor3 0
g = OpCompositeExtract %float finalColor3 1
b = OpCompositeExtract %float finalColor3 2
finalCol = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 r g b %float_1

OpStore %outColor finalCol

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Type/function declarations for IDs used directly in the function body
; (placeholders to ensure correct parsing in some assemblers)
; These names are not re-declared as per SPIR-V assembly syntax; they are used above directly.

SPIR-V Shader - Horizontal Stripes

View SPIR-V (5465 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: custom
; Bound: 9999
; Schema: 0

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

; Uniform block (UBO) types
%UBOType = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBOType
%_ptr_Uniform_Vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

; Pointer types for shader inputs/outputs
%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_Vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

; Function types
%_type_void = OpTypeVoid
%_type_function_void = OpTypeFunction %void

; Variables (interfaces)
%worldPosIn = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%normalIn   = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%uvIn       = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%tangentIn  = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%colorIn    = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%outColor   = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_vec4 Output
; Uniform block instance
%ubo = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform

; Decorations
OpDecorate %worldPosIn Location 0
OpDecorate %normalIn   Location 1
OpDecorate %uvIn       Location 2
OpDecorate %tangentIn  Location 3
OpDecorate %colorIn    Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor   Location 0

OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0
OpDecorate %UBOType Block

; Offsets within the std140 layout
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBOType 5 Offset 224

; Entry point
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPosIn %normalIn %uvIn %tangentIn %colorIn %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Declare the main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %_type_function_void
%mainLabel = OpLabel

; Local variables
; Load inputs
%worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %worldPosIn
%normal   = OpLoad %vec3 %normalIn
%uv       = OpLoad %vec2 %uvIn

; Normalize the interpolated normal
%nx = OpCompositeExtract %float %normal 0
%ny = OpCompositeExtract %float %normal 1
%nz = OpCompositeExtract %float %normal 2
%nx2 = OpFMul %float %nx %nx
%ny2 = OpFMul %float %ny %ny
%nz2 = OpFMul %float %nz %nz
%ln2 = OpFAdd %float %nx2 %ny2
%ln2 = OpFAdd %float %ln2 %nz2
%ln  = OpExtInst %float GLSLstd450 %ln2 Sqrt
%one = OpConstant %float 1.0
%invN = OpFDiv %float %one %ln
%nX = OpFMul %float %nx %invN
%nY = OpFMul %float %ny %invN
%nZ = OpFMul %float %nz %invN
%N  = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %nX %nY %nZ

; Load light position from UBO (lightPos is member 3)
%lightPtr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_Vec4 %ubo %_const3
%lightPosV = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPtr
%LPx = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPosV 0
%LPy = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPosV 1
%LPz = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPosV 2

; World position components
%wx = OpCompositeExtract %float %worldPos 0
%wy = OpCompositeExtract %float %worldPos 1
%wz = OpCompositeExtract %float %worldPos 2

; Light direction
%dx = OpFSub %float %LPx %wx
%dy = OpFSub %float %LPy %wy
%dz = OpFSub %float %LPz %wz
%dx2 = OpFMul %float %dx %dx
%dy2 = OpFMul %float %dy %dy
%dz2 = OpFMul %float %dz %dz
%ld2 = OpFAdd %float %dx2 %dy2
%ld2 = OpFAdd %float %ld2 %dz2
%ld  = OpExtInst %float GLSLstd450 Sqrt %ld2
%oneL = OpConstant %float 1.0
%invL = OpFDiv %float %oneL %ld
%LX = OpFMul %float %dx %invL
%LY = OpFMul %float %dy %invL
%LZ = OpFMul %float %dz %invL
%L  = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %LX %LY %LZ

; Diffuse term: max(dot(N, L), 0.0)
%dNX = OpCompositeExtract %float %N 0
%dNY = OpCompositeExtract %float %N 1
%dNZ = OpCompositeExtract %float %N 2
%Lx  = OpCompositeExtract %float %L 0
%Ly  = OpCompositeExtract %float %L 1
%Lz  = OpCompositeExtract %float %L 2
%dot1 = OpFMul %float %dNX %Lx
%dot2 = OpFMul %float %dNY %Ly
%dot3 = OpFMul %float %dNZ %Lz
%dot12 = OpFAdd %float %dot1 %dot2
%dot  = OpFAdd %float %dot12 %dot3
%zeroF = OpConstant %float 0.0
%diffuseRaw = OpExtInst %float GLSLstd450 Max %dot %zeroF

; Lambert factor = 0.4 + 0.6 * diffuse
%_0_4 = OpConstant %float 0.4
%_0_6 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%tmpLam = OpFMul %float %_0_6 %diffuseRaw
%lambert = OpFAdd %float %_0_4 % %tmpLam

; Stripes: sin(uv.y * pi * 16.0)
%_float_k = OpConstant %float 50.26544
%uvY = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv 1
%uvYk = OpFMul %float %uvY % %_float_k
%stripe = OpExtInst %float GLSLstd450 Sin %uvYk
; Step to get 0 or 1
%StripeEdge = OpConstant %float 0.0
%stepStripe = OpExtInst %float GLSLstd450 Step %StripeEdge %stripe

; Red and Blue stripe colors
%redVec = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %_float_0_9 %_float_0_1 %_float_0_1
%blueVec = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %_float_0_1 %_float_0_1 %_float_0_9
; Mix red and blue by stepStripe
%stripeCol = OpExtInst %vec3 GLSLstd450 Mix %redVec %blueVec %stepStripe

; Apply Lambert shading
%finalColor3 = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %stripeCol %lambert

; Output with alpha = 1.0
%alpha = OpConstant %float 1.0
%outColorVec4 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %finalColor3 %alpha
OpStore %outColor %outColorVec4

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Extra constants (used above)
%_const3 = OpConstant %uint 3
%uint = OpTypeInt 32 0
%_float_0_9 = OpConstant %float 0.9
%_float_0_1 = OpConstant %float 0.1
%_float_0_4 = OpConstant %float 0.4
%_float_0_6 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%_float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: handcrafted
; Bound: 9999
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%bool = OpTypeBool

; Input/output pointers
%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

; Uniform block types
%_mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4
%_struct_UBO = OpTypeStruct %_mat4 %_mat4 %_mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_structUBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %_struct_UBO
%_ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

; Variables (fragment inputs/outputs and uniform block)
%inNormal = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%inUV = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_vec4 Output
%ubo = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_structUBO Uniform

; Decorations
OpDecorate %inNormal Location 1
OpDecorate %inUV Location 2
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0
OpDecorate %_struct_UBO Block
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %_struct_UBO 5 Offset 224

; Entry point declarations
%main = OpFunction %void None %voidfn
%entry = OpLabel

; Constants
%const_6 = OpConstant %float 6.0
%const_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%const_0_3 = OpConstant %float 0.3
%const_0_7 = OpConstant %float 0.7
%const_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%const_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%const_0_1 = OpConstant %float 0.1
%const_0_09 = OpConstant %float 0.09
%const_0_57735 = OpConstant %float 0.57735
%const_0_8 = OpConstant %float 0.8

; Local: sample UV and compute 6x6 polka-dot pattern
%uv = OpLoad %vec2 %inUV
%uv6 = OpFMul %vec2 %uv %const_6
%uv6floor = OpFFloor %vec2 %uv6
%cell = OpFSub %vec2 %uv6 %uv6floor
%cellX = OpCompositeExtract %float %cell 0
%cellY = OpCompositeExtract %float %cell 1
%dx = OpFSub %float %cellX %const_0_5
%dy = OpFSub %float %cellY %const_0_5
%dx2 = OpFMul %float %dx %dx
%dy2 = OpFMul %float %dy %dy
%dist2 = OpFAdd %float %dx2 %dy2
%inside = OpFOrdLessThanEqual %bool %dist2 %%const_0_09

; Colors
%yellow = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %const_1 %const_0_8 %const_0
%blue   = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %const_0_1 %const_0_1 %const_0_3
%colorDot = OpSelect %vec3 %inside %yellow %blue

; Lambertian shading with fixed light direction (0.57735,0.57735,0.57735)
%n = OpLoad %vec3 %inNormal
%nx = OpCompositeExtract %float %n 0
%ny = OpCompositeExtract %float %n 1
%nz = OpCompositeExtract %float %n 2
%Lx = %const_0_57735
%Ly = %const_0_57735
%Lz = %const_0_57735
%dxL = OpFMul %float %nx %Lx
%dyL = OpFMul %float %ny %Ly
%dzL = OpFMul %float %nz %Lz
%dotNL = OpFAdd %float (OpFAdd %float %dxL %dyL) %dzL
%diffuse = OpFMax %float %dotNL %%const_0
%shadeMul = OpFMul %float %diffuse %const_0_7
%shade = OpFAdd %float %const_0_3 % %shadeMul

; Apply shading to color
%colored = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %colorDot %shade
%cr = OpCompositeExtract %float %colored 0
%cg = OpCompositeExtract %float %colored 1
%cb = OpCompositeExtract %float %colored 2
%outA = %const_1
%outCol = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %cr %cg %cb %outA
OpStore %outColor %outCol

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Function types
%voidfn = OpTypeFunction %void
%voidfn = OpTypeFunction %void
; End of module

View SPIR-V (3942 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: custom
; Bound: 9999
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
%GLSL_STD_450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4
%u32 = OpTypeInt 32 0

; Uniform block (std140: 3 mat4 + 3 vec4)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%_ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%_ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

; Pointers for inputs/outputs
%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

; Variables
%inWorldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%inNormal    = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%inUv        = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%inTangent   = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%inColor     = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%outColor    = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_vec4 Output
%ubo         = OpVariable %_ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform

; Decorations
OpDecorate %inWorldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %inNormal    Location 1
OpDecorate %inUv        Location 2
OpDecorate %inTangent   Location 3
OpDecorate %inColor     Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor    Location 0
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0
OpDecorate %UBO Block
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224

; Constants
%const_0_4 = OpConstant %float 0.4
%const_0_6 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%const_1_0 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%const_0_3 = OpConstant %float 0.3
%const_0_15 = OpConstant %float 0.15
%const_0_05 = OpConstant %float 0.05
%const_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%zero = OpConstant %float 0.0

; Color constants
%skyColor = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %const_0_4 %const_0_6 %const_1_0
%groundColor = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %const_0_3 %const_0_15 %const_0_05

; Small helpers
%idx3 = OpConstant %u32 3
; 1.0 float constant already defined as %const_1_0

; Function types
%void_fn = OpTypeFunction %void
%main = OpFunction %void None %void_fn
%entry = OpLabel

; Entry-point declaration
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %inWorldPos %inNormal %inUv %inTangent %inColor %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Load inputs
%worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %inWorldPos
%normalIn = OpLoad %vec3 %inNormal

; Normalize normal
%N = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSL_STD_450 Normalize %normalIn

; Hemisphere blend parameter t = N.y * 0.5 + 0.5
%Ny = OpCompositeExtract %float %N 1
%Ny_half = OpFMul %float %Ny %const_0_5
%t = OpFAdd %float %Ny_half %const_0_5
%oneMinusT = OpFSub %float %const_1_0 %t

; Ambient color = mix(skyColor, groundColor, t) = sky*(1-t) + ground*t
%ambientSky = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %skyColor %oneMinusT
%ambientGround = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %groundColor %t
%ambient = OpFAdd %vec3 %ambientSky %ambientGround

; Read light position from UBO (lightPos is member 3)
%lightPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %_ptr_Uniform_vec4 %ubo %idx3
%lightPosW = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPos_ptr
%lpX = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPosW 0
%lpY = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPosW 1
%lpZ = OpCompositeExtract %float %lightPosW 2
%lightPos3 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %lpX %lpY %lpZ

; Diffuse term
%toLight = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos3 %worldPos
%L = OpExtInst %vec3 %GLSL_STD_450 Normalize %toLight
%dotNL = OpExtInst %float %GLSL_STD_450 Dot %N %L
%d0 = OpExtInst %float %GLSL_STD_450 FMax %dotNL %zero
%diffuseScalar = OpFMul %float %d0 %const_0_3
%diffuseVec3 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %diffuseScalar %diffuseScalar %diffuseScalar
%ambientPlusDiffuse = OpFAdd %vec3 %ambient %diffuseVec3

; Output color with alpha = 1.0
%outColorVec4 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %ambientPlusDiffuse %const_1_0
OpStore %outColor %outColorVec4

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Khronos SPIR-V Tools
; Bound: 999
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

%void = OpTypeVoid
%func = OpTypeFunction %void
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %v4float 4

; Inputs
%ptr_input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%WorldPos = OpVariable %ptr_input_v3float Input
OpDecorate %WorldPos Location 0

; Outputs
%ptr_output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%outColor = OpVariable %ptr_output_v4float Output
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Uniform Block (UBO)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %v4float %v4float %v4float
%UBOPtr = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%ubo = OpVariable %UBOPtr Uniform
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0
OpDecorate %UBO Block
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224

; GLSL std450 for clamp
%GLSLstd450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

; Constants
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%float_0_1 = OpConstant %float 0.1
%float_0_4 = OpConstant %float 0.4
%float_0_6 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%float_0_9 = OpConstant %float 0.9
%float_0_? = OpConstant %float 0.0 ; placeholder (not used)
%float_0_?2 = OpConstant %float 0.0 ; placeholder (not used)
; Note: some placeholders above are not needed but kept to ensure unique IDs if expanded

; Entry point
%main = OpFunction %void None %func
%entry = OpLabel

; Load worldPos and compute t from worldPos.y
%pos = OpLoad %v3float %WorldPos
%y = OpCompositeExtract %float %pos 1
%yMul = OpFMul %float %y %float_0_5
%tAdd = OpFAdd %float %yMul %%float_0_5
%tClamped = OpExtInst %float %GLSLstd450 Clamp %tAdd %float_0 %float_1

; Color calculation: r = 0.1 + 0.9*t, g = 0.4 + 0.6*t, b = 0.1 + 0.9*t
%rMul = OpFMul %float %float_0_9 %tClamped
%r = OpFAdd %float %rMul % %float_0_1
%gMul = OpFMul %float %float_0_6 %tClamped
%g = OpFAdd %float %gMul % %float_0_4
%bMul = OpFMul %float %float_0_9 %tClamped
%b = OpFAdd %float %bMul % %float_0_1

; Compose final color with alpha = 1.0
%color = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %r %g %b %float_1
OpStore %outColor %color

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Entry point declaration
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %WorldPos %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4
; Uniform block (UBO)
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

; Input variables
%in_worldPos = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%in_normal   = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%in_uv       = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%in_tangent  = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
